Notice Of Motion To Reclassify Limited Civil Case As Unlimited Civil Case
Off Calendar Aug-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Emmett Gilman, Esq. of the Gilman Law Firm, T:415.653.0153, for plaintiff & moving party;
Raymond R. Rollan, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, T:415.554.3888, for defendants & opposing party.

Prior to the hearing, the Court stated on the record that although he conducted the Judicial Mediation proceedings in this case, the parties stipulated that he can hear the matter. There was no objection by either party.

The issue regarding the trial date was discussed and the Court instructed the parties to bring any request for trial continuance before Department 206.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS SHARON BROWN AND JAMILA SWAIN'S Motion To Reclassify Limited Civil Case As Unlimited Civil Case as follows:

Plaintiffs Sharon Brown and Jamila Swain's CCP Sec. 403.040 motion for reclassification of these consolidated cases from limited to unlimited jurisdiction is granted. Plaintiffs' counsel asserts that recent events have increased potential damages in the case. Most notably, in the past three months, Ms. Brown's back pain has caused reduction in her work schedule from five to three days a week, counsel avers.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS , CGC-17-558283 WITH CASE CGC-17-558282
Off Calendar Dec-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Samuel Ray, Esq., Deputy City Attonrey CCSF, Telephone 415-554-3868, appearing for Defendants Robert Beck, and CCSF;
No other appearance.

No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to consolidate case 558282 with case 558283 is granted. No opposition filed. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes. Case 558282 is the lead case and all further papers must be filed in that case only. The trial date of June 17, 2019 previously set for case 558283 now applies to both consolidated cases. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)
